title: Covalent Coupling Protein to Carboxylated Microparticles via EDAC
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"fki3i","text":"This protocol details how to covalently couple proteins to carboxylated polystyrene particles (microspheres) via EDAC. In this reaction, 1-Ethyl-3-(3-dimethylaminopropyl)carbodiimide (EDAC) serves as an agent for covalently coupling protein (e.g. peptides, IgG, etc.) to carboxyl-modified particles by forming an amide bond. Various 2-(N-morpholino)ethanesulfonic (MES) acid buffers are used to maintain the appropriate pH ranges. Glycine is used as a quencher to cease the reaction. Bovine serum albumin (BSA) is used as a blocking agent, to prevent non-specific interactions with and between particles (e.g. hydrophobic interactions).","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":336,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ec9s3","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8qjh4","text":"This protocol's development was guided by the information contained in Bangs Laboraties Inc. Tech Note 205, Covalent Coupling. This file is attached as a PDF.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"cvs3t","text":"When using fluorescent particles, avoid prolonged exposure to light as this may cause photobleaching.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"63ujh","text":"Ensure that all buffers are prepared. Take EDAC aliquot from deep freezer, and allow to reach room temperature.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Vortex particle mixture. Centrifuge at 9900 x g, °C, 10 minutes Remove supernatant. Resuspend in 500 µL Activation buffer. Repeat for a total of two washes; Do not resuspend in Activation Buffer the second time. All centrifugation times and speeds will depend on particle size. They will need to be adjusted accordingly.
Add 10 µL particles to 2 mL centrifuge tube. Add 500 µL Activation buffer.
Sonicate carboxylated polystyrene particle stock in sonicating bath for 00:10:00 . Vortex mix particle stock for 00:00:30 .

During the centrifugation times in Step 3, make EDAC solution: Add 5 mL Activation buffer to 5 mg EDAC aliquot. Vortex mix. This amount of EDAC is in excess. Tech Note 205 uses solution with100 mg EDAC for 100 mg particles. The excess EDAC is mostly removed by centrifugation.
Resuspend particle pellet from Step 3 with EDAC/Activation buffer, ensuring that particles are well suspended. Agitate on orbital mixer, 00:15:00 .
Vortex particle mixture. Centrifuge at 9900 x g, °C, 10 minutes . Remove supernatant. Resuspend in Coupling buffer. Repeat for a total of two washes.

During the centrifugation times in Step 6, make Protein solution: Add protein for 1-10X coverage (as calculated below) to 500 µL Coupling buffer. Vortex mix. The amount of protein (e.g. antibody, peptide) will depend on particle concentration, size, etc. The following equation is used to calculate the necessary amount (adapted from TechNote 205): S = \frac{6C}{ \rho d} S (mg protein / g particles): amount of protein for surface saturation C (mg protein / m 2 particle surface): capacity of particle surface for a given protein ρ (g / cm 3 ): density of particle d (μm): mean particle diameter.
Combine pellet suspension and Protein solution. Pipet mix. Agitate on orbital mixer, 02:00:00 to 04:00:00 at room temperature.
Centrifuge at 9900 x g, °C, 10 minutes . Remove supernatant. Resuspend particle pellet with 500 µL Coupling buffer Vortex mix. Centrifuge at 9900 x g, °C, 10 minutes .
Resuspend particle pellet from Step 9 with 1 mL Quenching solution. Agitate on orbital mixer, 00:30:00 at room temperature.
Centrifuge at 9900 x g, °C, 10 minutes . Remove supernatant. Resuspend particle pellet with 500 µL Blocking solution Agitate on orbital mixer, 00:15:00 at room temperature.
Centrifuge at 9900 x g, °C, 10 minutes . Remove supernatant. Resuspend particle pellet with 500 µL Storage buffer. Vortex mix.
Centrifuge at 9900 x g, °C, 20 minutes . Remove supernatant. Resuspend particle pellet with Storage buffer to desired storage concentration. Store at 4 °C .
creator: Kenneth Schackart
